channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Fed Holds Rates on Oil Inflation Fears; HDFC Bank Plunges on Governance Row
item.description: The Federal Reserve maintained its interest rates, signaling an extended pause due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and their inflationary impact. This move coincides with a sharp sell-off in HDFC Bank shares following its chairman's abrupt resignation over ethical concerns, highlighting significant governance risks amidst broader market volatility. India faces increased economic vulnerability from soaring oil prices and a weakening rupee.

item.title: TARC Unveils ₹3,600 Crore Luxury Project 'Ishvara' in Gurugram
item.description: TARC Limited has launched 'Ishvara,' a new luxury residential project in Gurugram's Sector 63A. The development has an estimated Gross Development Value (GDV) of ₹3,600 crore, spans over 9 acres, and includes 518 residences across six towers. This launch completes the TARC Ishva masterplan, boosting TARC's premium real estate portfolio.

item.title: CLC Industries Promoter Sells ₹22 Cr Stake; Holding Drops to 90%
item.description: CLC Industries promoter Manjeet Cotton sold 5,19,720 shares for ₹22.08 crore on March 17-18, 2026, reducing its stake from 95% to 90%. This follows the company's exit from CIRP, raising questions about promoter commitment amid financial challenges.

item.title: Sonali Commercial Buys 18,125 Shares in Chambal Fertilisers
item.description: Sonali Commercial Limited has acquired 18,125 shares in Chambal Fertilisers & Chemicals Limited through open market purchases, raising its total stake to 3,84,297 shares. This transaction represents a minor increase in Sonali Commercial's holding, which now stands at 0.09% of Chambal Fertilisers' total diluted share capital.

item.title: India Orders Oil Firms to Share Data to Control Supply
item.description: India now requires all oil and gas companies to submit detailed data to a central agency. This move strengthens the nation's energy security, allowing for quicker responses to volatile global prices and supply issues, especially given the ongoing severe cooking gas shortage.

item.title: Chambal Fertilisers: Shekhar Trust Buys Shares, Ups Stake to 3.46%
item.description: Shekhar Family Trust bought 2,08,515 shares in Chambal Fertilisers & Chemicals Ltd through open market purchases. This raises its total holding to 13,873,126 shares, or 3.46% of the company's voting capital. The transactions occurred March 17-18, 2026, signaling continued investor interest. While a small percentage increase, it shows confidence from a key stakeholder in the leading agri-inputs company.

item.title: Ecoplast Ltd. Halts Trading Ahead of FY26 Results
item.description: Ecoplast Ltd. has closed its trading window starting April 1, 2026, as required by SEBI insider trading rules. This restriction will last until 48 hours after the board approves the audited financial results for the year ending March 31, 2026. The board meeting date is still to be announced.

item.title: ITC Board Welcomes Navin Agarwal from April 1, 2026
item.description: ITC Limited's shareholders have approved the appointment of Mr. Navin Agarwal as a Non-Executive Director, effective April 1, 2026, for a three-year term. Mr. Agarwal brings a wealth of experience from government bodies and public sector enterprises to ITC's board. His induction is expected to further bolster the company's governance framework and strategic oversight.

item.title: Sammaan Capital Confirms ₹5.46 Crore NCD Interest Payment
item.description: Sammaan Capital Limited has confirmed it paid ₹5.46 crore in interest on its Secured Redeemable Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs). The company met its obligations for all 16 listed NCD series, reassuring debenture holders about its financial commitments.

item.title: Gallant Enterprise shareholders OK ₹4,000 Cr borrowing, promoter deals
item.description: Gallant Enterprise Limited's shareholders have approved boosting the company's borrowing limit to ₹4,000 crore and allowing charges on its properties up to that amount. They also approved key transactions with promoter firms GSEC Limited and Monarch Infraparks Private Limited, giving the company more financial flexibility for operations.

item.title: India's ₹497 Cr Export Aid Targets Rising Global Shipping Costs
item.description: India's trade ministry has launched a ₹497 crore ($53.26 million) plan to provide insurance cover for shipments through conflict zones. The initiative aims to lower sharply rising logistics costs, such as war-risk premiums and emergency surcharges, which are straining Indian SMEs and causing payment delays. The goal is to stabilize exporter expenses and stop order cancellations amid geopolitical disruptions affecting trade.

item.title: Prime Fresh Promoter Hardik Ghelani Pledges 4.55% Stake to Bank of Baroda
item.description: Prime Fresh Limited's promoter, Hardik Ghelani, has pledged 6,25,800 equity shares, representing 4.55% of the company's total share capital, as collateral for a loan from Bank of Baroda. The disclosure, made on March 19, 2026, ties a portion of the promoter's holding to loan repayment obligations, introducing a potential risk if the loan is not serviced.

item.title: Diamond Power shareholders OK ₹4,000 crore borrowing, promoter deals
item.description: Diamond Power Infrastructure Limited shareholders have approved key financial resolutions, including raising borrowing and property charge limits to ₹4,000 crore each. They also sanctioned material related-party transactions with promoter firms GSEC Limited and Monarch Infraparks Private Limited. This boosts the company's financial flexibility, though it faces past governance concerns and negative equity.

item.title: Welspun Living Boosts Anjar Power Capex 112%, Buys 35% WCSL Stake
item.description: Welspun Living Limited announced significant strategic moves, including a substantial increase in capital expenditure for renewable energy power transmission to its Anjar facility, which will now stand at ₹159.38 crore. The company also approved acquiring a 35% stake in Welspun Corporate Services Limited (WCSL) for ₹35,000, aiming to establish it as a group-wide corporate services platform. Key management transitions were also noted.

item.title: ZF Steering Gear Subsidiaries Consolidate Operations with MIDC Lease Deal
item.description: ZF Steering Gear (India) Limited announced that its subsidiaries, DriveSys Systems Private Limited and NexSteer Systems Private Limited, have finalized the assignment of leasehold rights for a plot at MIDC Supa Parner Industrial Park in Maharashtra. The Maharashtra Industrial Development Corporation (MIDC) has approved the transaction, which aims to consolidate operational assets and streamline processes for its units.

item.title: Welspun Living Acquires Service Arm Stake, Nearly Doubles Anjar CAPEX
item.description: Welspun Living's Board has approved acquiring a 35% stake in Welspun Corporate Services Limited (WCSL) for ₹35,000, making it an associate company. The company also significantly increased its capital expenditure for renewable energy power transmission to its Anjar facility, revising the outlay to ₹159.38 crore from ₹75 crore. These moves aim to enhance group-wide support services and bolster green energy infrastructure.

item.title: Bikaji Foods to Host Investor Meetings in Singapore March 25, 2026
item.description: Bikaji Foods International Limited announced it is holding one-on-one meetings with analysts and institutional investors in Singapore on March 25, 2026. The company will share only publicly available information, ensuring no unpublished price-sensitive data is disclosed. This engagement aims to foster investor relations and transparent communication.

item.title: HDFC Bank Board to Seek CEO Extension Amid Senior Departures
item.description: HDFC Bank's board is preparing to formally request an extension for CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan beyond his October term, driven by a critical need for leadership continuity amidst significant senior executive departures. Regulatory sources indicate the Reserve Bank of India favors this stability, essential for navigating post-merger integration and competitive market pressures. This strategic move aims to bolster investor confidence during a period of internal transition.

item.title: JK Cement Wins Limestone Block But Fuel Costs Squeeze Margins
item.description: JK Cement has won the bid for the Itauri-Jharkua limestone block in Madhya Pradesh, securing a vital long-term raw material supply. This news comes as the company reported a 23% year-on-year drop in net profit for Q3 FY26, despite higher revenue. The stock saw a slight dip as investors worried about rising input costs due to geopolitical tensions and tough competition. Analyst views are split, but the cement sector's long-term future looks bright thanks to government infrastructure spending.

item.title: Prudential Reviews India Stake, ICICI Pru Life Stock Falls
item.description: Shares of ICICI Bank and its life insurance arm, ICICI Prudential Life Insurance, experienced a notable decline following reports that Prudential Plc is examining its stake in the joint venture. This development comes amidst strong growth projections for India's insurance sector and Prudential's stated commitment to the Indian market, including new health insurance initiatives. While ICICI Prudential Life maintains its position as a leading insurer, the news has prompted investor caution, contrasting with the broader positive outlook for the industry. ICICI Bank's stock saw a reduction of over 1.6%, while ICICI Prudential Life Insurance shares fell by nearly 4%.

item.title: SG Finserve Raises ₹132 Crore By Converting Warrants, Boosting Capital
item.description: SG Finserve Limited secured ₹132.47 crore by issuing 39,25,000 new equity shares as investors exercised their warrants. This capital injection boosts the company's issued and paid-up capital, furthering its financial strategy.

item.title: Solitaire Machine Tools: Promoter Shifts 0.33% Stake in Off-Market Deal
item.description: Solitaire Machine Tools Limited has reported an off-market inter-se transfer of 15,000 equity shares, equivalent to 0.33% of its total equity. The shares were transferred by promoter Mr. Ashok Jivarajbhai Sheth to promoter Mrs. Swati Hemendra Badani. This transaction, executed on March 17, 2026, signifies a shift in shareholding within the promoter group but does not alter the overall promoter stake in the company.

item.title: Geopolitical Fears Drive Billions to Bitcoin ETFs, Away From Gold
item.description: Global tensions and rising oil prices are driving investors to seek safety, with billions flowing into Bitcoin ETFs while gold sees outflows. Bitcoin's 24/7 market access and institutional demand are positioning it as a growing, though still untested, hedge against uncertainty. Retail investors remain more cautious.

item.title: Bikaji Foods to Hold Hong Kong Investor Meetings March 27, 2026
item.description: Bikaji Foods International Limited will hold one-to-one meetings with analysts and institutional investors in Hong Kong on March 27, 2026. The company stated it will use publicly available investor presentations and will not disclose any Unpublished Price Sensitive Information (UPSI), calling it a routine investor outreach initiative.

item.title: DCM Ltd. Faces Arbitration With GCD Prime Over Haryana Project Dispute
item.description: DCM Limited is facing arbitration from developer GCD Prime over their Joint Development Agreement (JDA). The developer's move follows DCM's earlier termination notice, which cited delays and breaches related to a suspended project license in Haryana. The financial impact of this arbitration is currently unclear.

item.title: India Launches Export Insurance Fund to Counter Trade Route Risks
item.description: India's Ministry of Commerce and Industry is establishing a dedicated fund to provide insurance cover for exporters shipping through conflict-hit maritime corridors. This initiative aims to counter sharply increased logistics costs and the withdrawal of global reinsurers, driven by geopolitical disruptions in West Asia. The proposed facility will serve as a crucial reinsurance backstop, bolstering domestic insurers' capacity and ensuring the continuity of India's vital trade flows amidst heightened global risks.

item.title: Jefferies Backs GMR Airports, Groww; Manappuram Finance Faces Ownership Shift to Bain Capital
item.description: Jefferies maintains a 'Buy' on GMR Airports Infrastructure (₹125 target) on strong traffic and revenue growth, plus Hyderabad expansion plans. Groww also keeps a 'Buy' (₹195 target) driven by user loyalty and wealth products, targeting 33% CAGR. Manappuram Finance receives a 'Hold' (₹285 target) as Bain Capital's acquisition proceeds, with execution critical. NBFC sector growth moderates, but aviation and fintech remain resilient.

item.title: Gold Tumbles as Fed's 'Higher-for-Longer' Rates Trump Geopolitics
item.description: Gold has fallen to approximately $4,766 per ounce, marking a nearly six-week low and an 8% drop since the US-Israel conflict escalated. Persistent inflation data, including a 0.7% rise in February PPI and steady CPI, forces the Federal Reserve into a 'higher-for-longer' interest rate stance. This policy, coupled with a strengthening US Dollar Index above 100, is suppressing safe-haven demand for gold, overriding geopolitical concerns. Despite the Fed projecting 2.4% growth for 2026, revised Q4 2025 GDP of only 0.7% suggests underlying economic fragility.

item.title: Amagi Media Labs Faces ₹2.06 Cr Income Tax Demand; Company to Appeal
item.description: Amagi Media Labs Limited has received an Income Tax demand order totaling ₹2.06 crore, including interest, for the financial year 2019-20. The demand arises from allegations that the company failed to deduct tax on technical service fees paid to non-residents, classifying it as an "assessee in default." The company disputes the demand and is preparing to file an appeal, asserting that the resolution process will not impact its operations.

item.title: Bombay HC Rules: No 'Irrevocable' Clause for Charity Tax Registration
item.description: The Bombay High Court has ruled that charitable trusts do not need an explicit irrevocability clause in their deeds to obtain or renew registration under Section 12AB of the Income-tax Act. This decision overturns the Income Tax Department's strict stance, which had led to numerous rejections and caused significant compliance burdens. The court mandated reconsideration of affected applications, aiming to streamline tax exemptions for the non-profit sector.

item.title: Zoom Bets on AI and India to Drive Collaboration Growth
item.description: Zoom is strategically pivoting from its pandemic-era video conferencing dominance to become a comprehensive AI-powered enterprise collaboration platform. This evolution integrates tools for unified communications, customer service, and messaging, aiming to enhance both employee and customer experiences. India plays a dual critical role as a significant market opportunity and a talent hub, with substantial R&D centers contributing to global product development. However, this pivot faces intense pressure from bundled offerings by rivals Microsoft and Google, compelling Zoom to emphasize its unique co-creation philosophy and AI integration. Usage has stabilized post-pandemic, with casual users declining but enterprise engagement remaining steady.

item.title: India's Rupee Plunges to Record Low on RBI Intervention
item.description: India's rupee hit a record low of 92.89 against the dollar Thursday, driven by a strong dollar and global market volatility. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is intensifying intervention using forward contracts, boosting its net-short dollar position toward $100 billion. This aims to stabilize the rupee amid geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices.

item.title: JBM Auto Explains BSE Volume Surge, Confirms Compliance
item.description: JBM Auto Limited has responded to the BSE regarding a recent large increase in its share trading volume. The auto parts maker confirmed it has followed all SEBI disclosure rules and has not hidden any important information. The company stated the trading activity was entirely due to market conditions.

item.title: India Offers ₹500 Cr Relief to Middle East Exporters Amid Iran War
item.description: India's government has rolled out a ₹500 crore relief package, the RELIEF scheme, to shore up exports to the Middle East. Launched amidst trade disruptions from the Iran war, the package aims to stabilize shipments, lower insurance costs, and prevent order cancellations for exporters.

item.title: WeWork India Secures ₹475 Cr Hyderabad Managed Office Deal
item.description: WeWork India Management has secured a major Membership Agreement for a Managed Office with TMUS India Private Limited, valued at ₹475.49 crore. The 60-month contract is set to bolster WeWork India's expansion plans in Hyderabad, adding approximately 250,348 sq ft of workspace and 1,507 workstations, reinforcing its regional presence.

item.title: Macquarie Drops HDFC Bank from Buy List Over Governance Concerns
item.description: Macquarie has dropped HDFC Bank from its prime buy recommendations, citing apprehension over governance issues following Atanu Chakraborty's sudden departure. The brokerage anticipates potential near-term underperformance, even as it acknowledges robust fundamentals. This move follows a 4% stock decline, despite management reassurances regarding the bank's financial health and regulatory satisfaction.

item.title: Capri Global Capital Limits Rise to ₹9,550 Cr; Stable 'AA' Rating Maintained
item.description: Capri Global Capital Limited (CGCL) has seen its credit limits significantly boosted by Acuité Ratings & Research. The bank loan facility limit is now ₹9,550 crore, up from ₹7,050 crore, and the Non-Convertible Debenture (NCD) limit has been raised from ₹750 crore to ₹2,850 crore. These enhancements, affirmed with an 'ACUITE AA / Stable' rating, signal improved creditworthiness and expanded borrowing capacity for the diversified NBFC.

item.title: Tata Chair N. Chandrasekaran Honored with UK Knighthood for India-UK Business
item.description: Tata Group Chairman N. Chandrasekaran received an honorary UK knighthood for boosting India-UK business ties. The award recognizes Tata's significant investments in UK automotive, technology, and green sectors, supporting British industrial goals. Tata remains a vital economic partner through job creation and future-focused initiatives.
